1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and Timothy our brother. To the church of God in Corinth, and to all God's people throughout Achaia:
May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you grace and peace.
Praise be to the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercy and the God of all comfort.
He comforts us in all our troubles, so that we can comfort others in any trouble with the same comfort we ourselves receive from God.
For just as we share in the many sufferings of Christ, so also through Christ we share in much comfort.
If we suffer, it is for your comfort and salvation. If we are comforted, it is for your comfort, which helps you patiently bear the same sufferings we suffer.
Our hope for you is firm, because we know that just as you share in our sufferings, you also share in our comfort.
Brothers and sisters, we do not want you to be uninformed about the trouble we had in Asia. We were under such great pressure, far beyond our strength, that we even gave up hope of staying alive.
In our hearts we felt the sentence of death. But this happened so that we would not trust in ourselves, but in God, who raises the dead.
He rescued us from such deadly danger, and he will rescue us again. We have set our hope on him that he will keep on rescuing us,
as you help us by praying for us. Then many people will give thanks on our behalf for the gift of grace given to us in answer to many prayers.

Now this is what we are proud of, and our conscience agrees: we have lived in the world, and especially toward you, with honesty and godly sincerity. We have not depended on worldly wisdom, but on God's grace.
For we do not write you anything you cannot read and understand. And I hope that you will understand fully,
as you have understood us in part, so that you can be proud of us, just as we will be proud of you on the day of our Lord Jesus.
Because I was sure of this, I planned to visit you first, so that you might be blessed twice.
I planned to visit you on my way to Macedonia, and again on my way back, so that you could help me on my journey to Judea.
When I planned this, was I just being careless? Or do I make my plans in a worldly way, saying “Yes, yes” and “No, no” at the same time?
But as surely as God is faithful, our message to you is not “Yes” and “No.”
For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, who was preached among you by me, Silas, and Timothy, was not “Yes” and “No.” In him it has always been “Yes.”
For no matter how many promises God has made, they are all “Yes” in Christ. And so through him we say “Amen” to the glory of God.
Now it is God who makes both us and you stand firm in Christ. He anointed us,
set his mark of ownership on us, and put his Spirit in our hearts as a guarantee of what is to come.
I call God as my witness — and I stake my life on it — that it was to spare you that I did not return to Corinth.
Not that we rule over your faith, but we work together with you for your joy, because it is by faith that you stand firm.

2.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: So I made up my mind not to make another painful visit to you.
For if I make you sad, who is left to make me glad, except you whom I have made sad?
I wrote as I did so that when I came, I would not be made sad by the very people who ought to make me glad. I was sure that all of you would share my joy.
I wrote to you out of great trouble and pain in my heart, with many tears — not to make you sad, but to let you know how much I love you.
If anyone has caused sadness, he has not so much made me sad as he has, in some way, made all of you sad. I do not want to make it sound worse than it is.
The punishment that most of you gave him is enough for him.
Now, instead, you should forgive him and comfort him, so that he will not be overwhelmed by too much sadness.
So I beg you to show him that you really love him.
I wrote to test you and to see whether you would obey in everything.
Anyone you forgive, I also forgive. And whatever I have forgiven — if there was anything to forgive — I forgave it for your sake in the presence of Christ,
so that Satan would not get the better of us. For we know what he is up to.
When I went to Troas to preach the good news of Christ, and the Lord opened a door for me there,
I still had no peace of mind, because I did not find my brother Titus there. So I said goodbye to them and went on to Macedonia.
But thanks be to God, who always leads us in victory in Christ, and who through us spreads everywhere the sweet smell of the knowledge of him.
For we are to God the pleasing smell of Christ, both among those who are being saved and among those who are being lost.
To the one we are the smell of death leading to death; to the other we are the smell of life leading to life. And who is able to do such work?
Unlike so many, we do not sell the word of God for profit. Instead, we speak in Christ before God with sincerity, like people sent from God.

3.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Are we starting to praise ourselves again? Or do we need letters of recommendation to you, or from you, as some people do?
You yourselves are our letter, written on our hearts, known and read by everyone.

You show that you are a letter from Christ, the result of our work. This letter is written not with ink, but with the Spirit of the living God; not on tablets of stone, but on tablets of human hearts.
We have this confidence before God through Christ.
Not that we are able by ourselves to claim that anything comes from us. Our ability comes from God.
He has made us able to be servants of a new agreement — not of the written law, but of the Spirit. For the written law kills, but the Spirit gives life.
The law that brought death, carved in letters on stone, came with such glory that the people of Israel could not look straight at Moses' face, because of its glory, even though that glory was fading.
Will not the work of the Spirit have even greater glory?
If the work that brought condemnation had glory, how much greater is the glory of the work that makes people right with God!
In fact, what was once glorious has no glory now, compared with the far greater glory.
And if what was fading away came with glory, how much greater is the glory of what lasts!
So, since we have such a hope, we are very bold.
We are not like Moses, who put a veil over his face so that the people of Israel could not see the end of what was fading away.
But their minds were made dull. To this day the same veil remains when they read the old agreement. It has not been lifted, because only in Christ is it taken away.
Yes, to this day, whenever Moses is read, a veil covers their hearts.
But whenever anyone turns to the Lord, the veil is taken away.
Now the Lord is the Spirit, and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom.
And all of us, with faces no longer veiled, see the glory of the Lord as if in a mirror. We are being changed into his likeness, with ever greater glory, which comes from the Lord, who is the Spirit.

4.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: So, since God in his mercy has given us this work, we do not give up.
Instead, we have given up shameful, secret ways. We do not use tricks, and we do not twist the word of God. By clearly telling the truth, we let everyone's conscience judge us in the sight of God.
And even if our good news is hidden, it is hidden to those who are being lost.
The god of this world has blinded the minds of those who do not believe, so that they cannot see the light of the good news about the glory of Christ, who is the image of God.
For we do not preach ourselves, but Jesus Christ as Lord, and ourselves as your servants for Jesus' sake.
For God, who said, Let light shine out of darkness, has made his light shine in our hearts, to give us the light of the knowledge of God's glory shining in the face of Jesus Christ.
But we have this treasure in clay jars, to show that this all-surpassing power is from God and not from us.
We are pressed on every side, but not crushed; we are confused, but we do not give up;
we are hunted, but not left alone; we are knocked down, but not destroyed.
We always carry around in our bodies the death of Jesus, so that the life of Jesus may also be seen in our bodies.
For we who are alive are always being handed over to death for Jesus' sake, so that the life of Jesus may be seen in our bodies, which will die.
So death is at work in us, but life is at work in you.
The Scripture says: I believed, and so I spoke. With that same spirit of faith, we also believe, and so we speak,
because we know that the One who raised the Lord Jesus from death will also raise us with Jesus and bring us, together with you, into his presence.
All this is for your benefit, so that the grace that is reaching more and more people may cause thanksgiving to overflow, to the glory of God.
So we do not give up. Even though our bodies are wearing out, our spirits are being made new day by day.
For our troubles are light and last only a short time. Yet they are gaining for us a glory that is far greater and lasts forever.
So we do not look at what can be seen, but at what cannot be seen. For what can be seen lasts only a short time, but what cannot be seen lasts forever.

5.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: We know that if the earthly tent we live in is taken down, we have a building from God — an eternal house in heaven, not built by human hands.
Meanwhile we groan, longing to be clothed with our heavenly home,
because when we are clothed, we will not be found naked.
While we are in this tent, we groan and feel weighed down. It is not that we want to be unclothed, but to be clothed with our heavenly home, so that what dies will be swallowed up by life.
It is God who has prepared us for this very thing, and he has given us his Spirit as a guarantee of what is to come.
So we are always confident. We know that as long as we are at home in the body, we are away from the Lord.
For we live by faith, not by sight.
So we are confident, and we would rather be away from the body and at home with the Lord.
So we make it our goal to please him, whether we are at home in the body or away from it.
For we must all stand before Christ to be judged, so that each of us may receive what is due for the things done while in the body, whether good or bad.
So, because we know what it means to fear the Lord, we try to persuade people. What we are is plain to God, and I hope it is also plain to your consciences.

We are not trying to praise ourselves to you again. Instead, we are giving you a reason to be proud of us, so that you can answer those who take pride in appearances rather than in what is in the heart.
If we are out of our minds, it is for God; if we are in our right minds, it is for you.
For the love of Christ controls us, because we are convinced that one died for all, and so all died.
And he died for all, so that those who live should no longer live for themselves, but for him who died for them and was raised again.
So from now on we do not judge anyone by human standards. Even though we once thought of Christ in that way, we do so no longer.
So if anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation. The old has gone; the new has come!
All this is from God, who brought us back to himself through Christ and gave us the work of bringing others back to him.
That is, God was in Christ, bringing the world back to himself, not counting people's sins against them. And he has trusted us with the message of how to be brought back to him.
So we are Christ's messengers, as though God were making his appeal through us. On Christ's behalf we beg you: Be brought back to God!
God made the one who never sinned to be sin for us, so that in him we might become the rightness of God.

6.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: As God's fellow workers, we beg you not to receive God's grace and then waste it.
For God says: At the right time I heard you, and on the day of salvation I helped you. I tell you, now is the right time; now is the day of salvation.
We do not put anything in anyone's way, so that no one can find fault with our work.
Instead, in everything we show that we are God's servants: by great patience in troubles, hardships, and disasters;
in beatings, imprisonments, and riots; in hard work, sleepless nights, and going without food;
by being pure, by knowledge, by patience, by kindness, by the Holy Spirit, and by sincere love;
by speaking the truth, by the power of God; with weapons of right living in the right hand and in the left;
through honor and dishonor, through bad reports and good reports. We are treated as liars, yet we are truthful;
as unknown, yet we are well known; as dying, and yet we live on; as beaten, and yet not killed;
as sad, yet always rejoicing; as poor, yet making many rich; as having nothing, yet owning everything.
We have spoken openly to you, dear Corinthians, and opened our hearts wide to you.
We are not holding back our love for you, but you are holding back your love for us.
I am speaking to you as my children: open your hearts wide to us in return.
Do not join together with unbelievers as if you were a matched pair. For what do right living and evil have in common? Or what does light share with darkness?
What agreement can there be between Christ and the devil? Or what does a believer share with an unbeliever?

And what does God's temple have in common with idols? For we are the temple of the living God. As God has said: I will live with them and walk among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
So: Come out from among them and be separate, says the Lord. Do not touch anything unclean, and I will welcome you.
And: I will be a Father to you, and you will be my sons and daughters, says the Lord Almighty.

7.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Dear friends, since we have these promises, let us make ourselves clean from everything that dirties the body and the spirit. Let us aim for complete holiness out of respect for God.
Open your hearts to us. We have wronged no one, ruined no one, and cheated no one.
I am not saying this to blame you. As I said before, you are in our hearts, so that we would live or die together with you.
I speak to you very openly, and I am very proud of you. I am greatly encouraged, and in all our troubles my joy knows no limits.
Even when we came to Macedonia, we found no rest. We were troubled on every side — fights on the outside, fears on the inside.
But God, who comforts those who are discouraged, comforted us by the arrival of Titus.
And not only by his coming, but also by the comfort you had given him. He told us how much you long for me, how sorry you are, and how concerned you are for me. So I was happier than ever.
Even though my letter made you sad, I do not regret it. I did regret it for a while, because I see that the letter hurt you, but only for a little while.
Now I am glad — not because you were made sad, but because your sadness led you to change your hearts. You became sad in a way that pleases God, so that we did not harm you in any way.
The kind of sadness that pleases God leads to a change of heart and to salvation, and leaves no regret. But the sadness of the world leads to death.

See what this godly sadness has produced in you: how serious you became, how eager to clear yourselves, how angry at the wrong, how alarmed, how full of longing, how concerned, how ready to see justice done. In every way you have shown yourselves to be clear in this matter.
So even though I wrote to you, it was not mainly because of the one who did the wrong, or the one who was hurt. I wrote so that you could see for yourselves, before God, how much you care for us.
By all this we are encouraged. And besides our own comfort, we were even happier to see how glad Titus was, because his spirit had been refreshed by all of you.
I had bragged to him about you, and you did not let me down. Everything we told you was true, and our bragging to Titus has also proven true.
And his love for you is even greater when he remembers how all of you obeyed, welcoming him with respect and care.
I am glad that I can have complete confidence in you.

8.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Brothers and sisters, we want you to know about the grace that God has given to the churches in Macedonia.
In the middle of a hard time of trouble, their overflowing joy and their deep poverty overflowed into rich generosity.
I can tell you that they gave as much as they could, and even more than they could, completely on their own.
They begged us again and again for the honor of sharing in this service to God's people.
And they did not do only what we hoped. First they gave themselves to the Lord, and then to us, because that is what God wanted.
So we urged Titus, since he had already started, to finish this work of grace among you.
You do well in everything — in faith, in speaking, in knowledge, in your eagerness, and in your love for us. So make sure you also do well in this work of grace.
I am not commanding you. But I am testing how real your love is, by comparing it with the eagerness of others.
For you know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ: though he was rich, for your sake he became poor, so that through his poverty you might become rich.
Here is my advice about what is best for you: Last year you were the first not only to give, but also to want to give.
So now finish the work, so that your eagerness to do it may be matched by your finishing it, according to what you have.
For if you are willing, the gift is welcome according to what you have, not according to what you do not have.
I do not mean that others should be relieved while you are burdened. The goal is fairness.
Right now your plenty will supply what they need, so that later their plenty may supply what you need. In this way things will be fair,
as the Scripture says: The one who gathered much did not have too much, and the one who gathered little did not have too little.
Thanks be to God, who put into the heart of Titus the same deep care for you that I have.
He welcomed our appeal, and being so eager, he is coming to you on his own.
And we are sending with him the brother who is praised by all the churches for his work in spreading the good news.
What is more, this brother was chosen by the churches to travel with us as we carry this gift, which we are handling to honor the Lord and to show our eagerness to help.
We want to avoid any blame in the way we handle this large gift.
For we are careful to do what is right, not only in the eyes of the Lord, but also in the eyes of people.
And we are sending with them our brother, who has often proven to us in many ways that he is eager, and who is now even more eager because of his great confidence in you.
As for Titus, he is my partner and fellow worker among you. As for the other brothers, they are representatives of the churches, an honor to Christ.
So show these men the proof of your love, and show the churches why we are proud of you.

9.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: I really do not need to write to you about this service to God's people,

because I know how eager you are to help. I have been bragging about it to the people of Macedonia, telling them that you in Achaia were ready a year ago. And your eagerness has stirred up most of them.
But I am sending the brothers so that our bragging about you will not be empty in this matter, and so that you will be ready, as I said you would be.
Otherwise, if any Macedonians come with me and find you unprepared, we — not to say you — would be ashamed for being so sure of you.
So I thought it necessary to urge the brothers to go ahead and finish getting ready the generous gift you had promised. Then it will be ready as a generous gift, not as something forced.
Remember this: whoever plants only a little will harvest only a little, and whoever plants a lot will harvest a lot.
Each person should give what he has decided in his heart to give — not unwillingly or because he has to, for God loves a cheerful giver.
And God is able to give you more grace than you need, so that in all things, at all times, you will have everything you need and plenty left over for every good work.
As the Scripture says: He scattered his gifts to the poor; his good deeds last forever.
Now God, who gives seed to the farmer and bread for food, will supply and increase your store of seed, and will make the harvest of your good deeds grow.
You will be made rich in every way, so that you can be generous on every occasion. And through us, your gift will cause people to thank God.
For this service you give not only meets the needs of God's people, but also overflows in many prayers of thanks to God.

Because of this service, by which you have proven yourselves, people will give glory to God. They will praise him because you obey the good news of Christ, which you say you believe, and because you share so generously with them and with everyone.
And they will pray for you with deep love, because of the wonderful grace God has given you.
Thanks be to God for his gift that is too wonderful for words!

10.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: By the gentleness and kindness of Christ, I, Paul, make my appeal to you — I, the one who is supposedly “shy” when face to face with you, but “bold” when away.
I beg you that, when I come, I will not have to be as bold as I expect to be toward some people who think we live in a worldly way.
For though we live in the world, we do not fight the way the world fights.
The weapons we fight with are not the world's weapons. Instead, they have God's power to tear down strongholds.
We tear down arguments and every proud thing that sets itself up against the knowledge of God, and we take every thought captive and make it obey Christ.
And we are ready to punish every act of disobedience, once your obedience is complete.
You are looking at things only on the surface. If anyone is sure that he belongs to Christ, he should remember that we belong to Christ just as much as he does.
So even if I brag a little too much about the authority the Lord gave us — authority to build you up, not to tear you down — I will not be ashamed.
I do not want to seem like I am only trying to scare you with my letters.
For some say: His letters are weighty and forceful, but in person he is weak and his speaking is nothing.
Such people should realize that what we are in our letters when we are away, we will be in our actions when we are there.
We would not dare to class or compare ourselves with some who praise themselves. When they measure themselves by themselves and compare themselves with one another, they are not wise.
We, however, will not brag beyond proper limits, but only within the area God has given us, which reaches even to you.
We are not going too far, as we would be if we had not reached you. In fact, we were the first to come to you with the good news of Christ.
We do not brag beyond our limits about work others have done. Our hope is that, as your faith grows, our work among you will greatly expand,
so that we can preach the good news in the regions beyond you. We do not want to brag about work already done in someone else's area.
But: Whoever wants to brag should brag about the Lord.
For it is not the one who praises himself who is approved, but the one whom the Lord praises.

11.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: I hope you will put up with a little of my foolishness. Yes, please put up with me.
I am jealous for you with a godly jealousy. I promised you to one husband, to Christ, so that I could present you to him as a pure bride.
But I am afraid that, just as the snake fooled Eve by its trickery, your minds may be led away from your sincere and pure love for Christ.

For if someone comes and preaches a different Jesus than the one we preached, or if you receive a different spirit than the one you received, or a different “good news” than the one you accepted, you put up with it easily enough.
But I do not think I am in any way less than those “super-apostles.”
I may not be a trained speaker, but I do have knowledge. We have made this perfectly clear to you in every way.
Was it a sin for me to lower myself in order to lift you up, by preaching the good news of God to you free of charge?
I took pay from other churches, robbing them, so to speak, in order to serve you.

And when I was with you and needed something, I was not a burden to anyone, because the brothers who came from Macedonia gave me what I needed. I have kept myself from being a burden to you in any way, and I will keep on doing so.
As surely as the truth of Christ is in me, no one in the regions of Achaia will stop me from being proud of this.
Why? Because I do not love you? God knows I do!
And I will keep on doing what I am doing, in order to cut the ground out from under those who want a chance to be seen as our equals in the things they brag about.
For such people are false apostles, dishonest workers, who pretend to be apostles of Christ.
And no wonder, for Satan himself pretends to be an angel of light.
So it is no surprise if his servants also pretend to be servants of right living. In the end they will get what their actions deserve.
I say again: let no one think I am a fool. But if you do, then accept me as a fool, so that I too may do a little bragging.
What I am saying now, I am not saying as the Lord would, but as a fool would, in this confident bragging.
Since many people brag in a worldly way, I will brag too.
You gladly put up with fools, since you are so wise!
In fact, you put up with anyone who makes you a slave, takes advantage of you, traps you, looks down on you, or slaps you in the face.
To my shame, I admit that we were too “weak” for that! But whatever anyone else dares to brag about — I am speaking as a fool — I dare to brag about it too.
Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I.
Are they servants of Christ? I am out of my mind to talk like this, but I am even more. I have worked much harder, been in prison more often, been beaten worse, and been near death again and again.
Five times the Jews gave me thirty-nine lashes.
Three times I was beaten with rods. Once I was stoned. Three times I was shipwrecked, and I spent a night and a day in the open sea.

I have been on many journeys, in danger from rivers, from robbers, from my own people, and from those who are not Jews. I have been in danger in the city, in the country, and at sea, and in danger from people pretending to be believers.
I have worked hard and struggled, often gone without sleep, known hunger and thirst, often gone without food, and been cold and without clothes.
Besides everything else, I face daily pressure: my concern for all the churches.
Who is weak without my feeling weak? Who is led into sin without my burning inside?
If I must brag, I will brag about the things that show my weakness.
The God and Father of the Lord Jesus, who is blessed forever, knows that I am not lying.
In Damascus, the governor under King Aretas had the city guarded in order to arrest me.
But I was lowered in a basket through a window in the wall, and so I escaped his hands.

12.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: I have to keep bragging, even though there is nothing to gain by it. So I will go on to visions and revelations from the Lord.
I know a man in Christ who, fourteen years ago, was caught up to the third heaven. Whether it was in his body or out of his body, I do not know; God knows.
And I know that this man — whether in the body or out of the body I do not know, but God knows —
was caught up to paradise. He heard things that cannot be told, things that no one is allowed to say.
I will brag about a man like that, but I will not brag about myself, except about my weaknesses.
Even if I did want to brag, I would not be a fool, because I would be telling the truth. But I hold back, so that no one will think more of me than what he sees in me or hears from me.
To keep me from becoming proud because of these wonderful revelations, I was given a thorn in my body, a messenger of Satan, to torment me and keep me from becoming proud.
Three times I begged the Lord to take it away from me.
But he said to me: My grace is all you need, for my power is made perfect in weakness. So I will gladly brag all the more about my weaknesses, so that the power of Christ may rest on me.
That is why, for Christ's sake, I am content with weaknesses, insults, hardships, troubles, and difficulties. For when I am weak, then I am strong.
I have made a fool of myself, but you made me do it. You should have been the ones speaking well of me. For I am not in any way less than those “super-apostles,” even though I am nothing.
The marks of a true apostle — signs, wonders, and miracles — were done among you with great patience.
How were you treated worse than the other churches, except that I was never a burden to you? Forgive me this wrong!

Now I am ready to come to you for the third time, and I will not be a burden to you. For I do not want what you have; I want you. After all, children should not have to save up for their parents, but parents for their children.
So I will very gladly spend everything I have, and even spend myself, for you. If I love you more, will you love me less?
Be that as it may, I was not a burden to you. But, you say, I was tricky and trapped you by trickery!
Did I take advantage of you through any of the people I sent to you?
I urged Titus to go to you, and I sent our brother with him. Did Titus take advantage of you? Did we not act in the same spirit and follow the same path?
Have you been thinking all this time that we are just defending ourselves to you? We speak before God, as those who belong to Christ. And everything we do, dear friends, is to build you up.

For I am afraid that when I come, I may not find you as I want you to be, and that you may not find me as you want me to be. I am afraid there may be arguing, jealousy, anger, selfishness, gossip, pride, and disorder.

I am afraid that when I come again, my God will make me feel low before you, and that I will grieve over many who sinned earlier and have not changed their hearts about the impurity, sexual sin, and wild living they took part in.

13.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: This will be my third visit to you. Every matter must be settled by the word of two or three witnesses.
I already warned those who sinned earlier and all the others, when I was with you the second time. And now, while I am away, I warn them again: when I come back, I will not spare anyone.
You want proof that Christ is speaking through me. He is not weak in dealing with you; he is powerful among you.
For he was crucified in weakness, yet he lives by the power of God. We too are weak in him, yet by God's power we will live with him to serve you.
Examine yourselves to see whether you are in the faith; test yourselves. Don't you know that Jesus Christ is in you? — unless, of course, you fail the test.
And I hope you will see that we have not failed the test.

Now we pray to God that you will not do anything wrong. We do not pray this so that people will see that we have passed the test, but so that you will do what is right, even if it looks as though we have failed.
For we cannot do anything against the truth, but only for the truth.
We are glad whenever we are weak but you are strong. And our prayer is that you may be fully mature.
This is why I write these things while I am away, so that when I come, I will not have to be harsh in using the authority the Lord gave me — authority to build you up, not to tear you down.
Finally, brothers and sisters, goodbye. Aim for full maturity, listen to my appeal, agree with one another, and live in peace. Then the God of love and peace will be with you.
Greet one another with a holy kiss.
All God's people here send you greetings.
May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with all of you. Amen.
